rows.name: Hamilton
rows.fixNote: re-identified as City of Hamilton (was mislabeled Township/Northumberland); pop corrected 11,059→569,353 (StatCan 2021 CSD); township split to ON-2515

curatedFacts.landmarks: Dundurn Castle, Royal Botanical Gardens, HMCS Haida, Art Gallery of Hamilton, Hamilton Waterfront Trail
curatedFacts.notable: known as 'The Ambitious City' and 'Waterfall Capital of the World' — more than 100 waterfalls cascade along the Niagara Escarpment within city limits
curatedFacts.economy: steel industry (Stelco, Dofasco/ArcelorMittal), healthcare (McMaster Medical Centre, St. Joseph's), advanced manufacturing, arts, education
curatedFacts.entityDesc: Hamilton is a township in Central Ontario, Ontario, Canada, with a population of approximately 11,059. Steel industry (stelco, dofasco/arcelormittal), healthcare (mcmaster medical centre, st. joseph's), advanced manufacturing, arts, education.
curatedFacts.longDesc:
Hamilton isn't just a township — it's a place where ambition meets opportunity. Founded founded by George Hamilton in 1816, this community has transformed into a thriving community of ~570,000 people.

What makes Hamilton remarkable for {BUSINESS_TYPE}? Character and community. The area encompasses everything from McMaster University, Bruce Trail, Gage Park, International Village, vibrant James Street North arts district, Locke Street boutiques. Each area has its own character, its own needs, and its own expectations.

The local economy is driven by steel industry (Stelco, Dofasco/ArcelorMittal), healthcare (McMaster Medical Centre, St. Joseph's), advanced manufacturing, arts, education. This economic foundation creates steady demand for services and supports a community that takes pride in maintaining its properties and infrastructure.

Community features include Dundurn Castle, Royal Botanical Gardens, HMCS Haida, Art Gallery of Hamilton, Hamilton Waterfront Trail, which give Hamilton its distinctive character.

Hamilton benefits from its location in Central Ontario. Whether you need routine {SERVICE} maintenance or a major project, {COMPANY_NAME} understands what Hamilton properties need. We've invested the time to learn this community — the local building codes, the weather patterns, the neighbourhood expectations. That's why Hamilton residents keep choosing {COMPANY_NAME}.

Our promise: transparent pricing, professional execution, results that last. Call {PHONE} for your {ESTIMATE_TYPE} estimate today.

curatedFacts.founded: 1876, named after St. Catharine of Alexandria
curatedFacts.landmarks: Welland Canal, Port Dalhousie, Montebello Park, Morningstar Mill, Brock University, FirstOntario Performing Arts Centre
curatedFacts.notable: known as 'The Garden City' — heart of the Niagara wine region with award-winning wineries, charming Port Dalhousie waterfront, and access to world-class attractions
curatedFacts.economy: wine industry (Niagara wine region), education (Brock University), tourism, automotive parts, food processing
curatedFacts.entityDesc: St. Catharines is a city in Hamilton-Niagara, Ontario, Canada, with a population of approximately 136,803. Wine industry (niagara wine region), education (brock university), tourism, automotive parts, food processing.
curatedFacts.longDesc:
St. Catharines isn't just Ontario's city — it's a place where ambition meets opportunity. Founded named after St. Catharine of Alexandria, this city has transformed into a thriving community of over ~137,000 people.

What makes St. Catharines remarkable for {BUSINESS_TYPE}? Character and community. The city encompasses everything from Welland Canal ship-watching, Niagara wine trail, Royal Henley Regatta, Grape and Wine Festival, Port Dalhousie carousel. Each neighborhood has its own character, its own needs, and its own expectations.

The local economy is driven by wine industry (Niagara wine region), education (Brock University), tourism, automotive parts, food processing. This economic foundation creates steady demand for services and supports a community that takes pride in maintaining its properties and infrastructure.

Community features include Welland Canal, Port Dalhousie, Montebello Park, Morningstar Mill, Brock University, FirstOntario Performing Arts Centre, which give St. Catharines its distinctive character.

curatedFacts.founded: 1904, named after the world-famous waterfalls (Haudenosaunee origin)
curatedFacts.landmarks: Niagara Falls (Horseshoe, American, Bridal Veil), Clifton Hill, Skylon Tower, Fallsview Casino, Journey Behind the Falls
curatedFacts.notable: home to one of the most iconic natural wonders on Earth — the city balances world-class tourism with a vibrant residential community
curatedFacts.economy: tourism (14 million+ annual visitors), gaming (Fallsview Casino, Casino Niagara), hydroelectric power, hospitality
curatedFacts.entityDesc: Niagara Falls is a city in Hamilton-Niagara, Ontario, Canada, with a population of approximately 94,415. Tourism (14 million+ annual visitors), gaming (fallsview casino, casino niagara), hydroelectric power, hospitality.
curatedFacts.longDesc:
Niagara Falls isn't just Ontario's city — it's a place where ambition meets opportunity. Founded named after the world-famous waterfalls (Haudenosaunee origin), this city has transformed into a thriving community of ~95,000 people.

What makes Niagara Falls remarkable for {BUSINESS_TYPE}? Character and community. The city encompasses everything from Niagara Parks, Botanical Gardens, Whirlpool Jet Boat Tours, Niagara Skywheel, Bird Kingdom, IMAX Theatre. Each neighborhood has its own character, its own needs, and its own expectations.

The local economy is driven by tourism (14 million+ annual visitors), gaming (Fallsview Casino, Casino Niagara), hydroelectric power, hospitality. This economic foundation creates steady demand for services and supports a community that takes pride in maintaining its properties and infrastructure.

Community features include Niagara Falls (Horseshoe, American, Bridal Veil), Clifton Hill, Skylon Tower, Fallsview Casino, Journey Behind the Falls, which give Niagara Falls its distinctive character.

Niagara Falls benefits from its location in Hamilton-Niagara. Whether you need routine {SERVICE} maintenance or a major project, {COMPANY_NAME} understands what Niagara Falls properties need.
